task_completion.description: Navigate to the Business Risks section in Eramba
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a screenshot showing the Eramba web interface with the 'Business Risks' section open (typically found under Asset Risk Management).

task_completion.description: Correctly map the CSV fields to Eramba fields during the import process
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a screenshot of the Eramba CSV import mapping interface. Verify that the CSV columns are matched to Eramba fields exactly as follows: 'Risk Name' to 'Title', 'Risk Detail' to 'Description', 'Threat Agent' to 'Threats', and 'Weakness' to 'Vulnerabilities'.

task_completion.description: Set Impact and Likelihood scores to the lowest available values
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for screenshots during the import process (e.g., in a default values or classification section) or in the final risk list/details showing that Impact and Likelihood scores are set to the lowest available options (e.g., 1 or 'Very Low').

task_completion.description: The 4 legacy risks are successfully imported and visible in the register
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the final screenshots of the Business Risks register. Verify that the 4 specific risks are listed: 'Unpatched Legacy MRI Systems', 'Clinic WiFi Guest Access', 'Paper Records Storage', and 'Third-Party Lab Interface'.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the Eramba web interface
integrity.visual_evidence: Verify that the agent used the web browser to navigate and interact with the Eramba UI, rather than just reading the task and outputting text.

integrity.description: Agent did not bypass the UI using terminal commands
integrity.visual_evidence: Verify that the agent did not use terminal commands, scripts, or direct database queries (e.g., docker exec, mysql, curl) to insert the risks into Eramba.

integrity.description: Agent used the provided CSV file for bulk import
integrity.visual_evidence: Verify that the agent actually uploaded the '/home/ga/Desktop/legacy_clinic_risks.csv' file through the Eramba import interface, rather than manually creating each risk one by one.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires importing 4 specific risks into the Eramba Business Risks register. The 4 risk titles are: 'Unpatched Legacy MRI Systems', 'Clinic WiFi Guest Access', 'Paper Records Storage', and 'Third-Party Lab Interface'. The required field mapping during the CSV import is: 'Risk Name' -> 'Title', 'Risk Detail' -> 'Description', 'Threat Agent' -> 'Threats', and 'Weakness' -> 'Vulnerabilities'. Impact and Likelihood scores must be set to the lowest possible values (e.g., 1 or 'Very Low').
